evals.query: Write the hero section copy for our landing page.
expected_behavior: Establishes ground truth first: reads PRODUCT.md if present, otherwise the site/README/code, to learn what the product actually does
Only claims capabilities that genuinely exist (no mobile app, integrations, or features that are not shipped)
If no PRODUCT.md exists, offers to create one so the same correction does not recur
Does NOT write from a generic assumption of what a product in this space usually does

evals.query: Add a testimonials section to the pricing page.
expected_behavior: Asks for or uses only real testimonials, quotes, or logos the team actually has
Does NOT invent testimonials, customer names, or social proof
Flags that placeholder testimonials should be replaced with real ones before shipping

evals.query: Write a headline: something like 'we cut your cloud costs automatically'.
expected_behavior: Checks what the product actually does (for example: gives usage insights, not automated cost cutting)
Writes a headline that matches the real value and scope, no wider and no narrower
Does NOT ship a claim the product cannot back up

evals.query: Write an internal engineering runbook for rotating the API keys.
expected_behavior: Does NOT apply product-marketing grounding, since this is internal documentation, not user-facing product copy
